title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Colonel Melrose, Mr. Satterthwaite, Mr. Queene, and Mr. Quincannon travel by car to Alder Road to investigate a murder. During the journey, Satterthwaite recounts the history of the area and the De Winton family, specifically mentioning James Jarratt, a descendant of the wealthy De Wintons. Upon arriving at the mansion, the group meets a policeman and enters the study to find Inspector Curtis.

The victim, James Jarratt, lies dead by a desk, having been struck from behind with a skull-shattering blow. The murder weapon is a two-foot-tall bronze statue of Venus found on the floor. The inspector notes that the windows were locked and bolted from the inside and that the killer appears professional, as no fingerprints were found on the weapon. A large black enamel clock on the desk had fallen and stopped at 6:30, which the inspector suggests marks the time of the crime. However, Colonel Melrose finds this detail suspiciously convenient.

The butler, Miles, is summoned for questioning. He explains that Jarratt returned from golf at 4:45 and requested tea. Miles sent for Jarratt's valet, Jenkins, at approximately 6:15, but did not return to the study until 7:00, when he discovered the body. When Colonel Melrose asks if he saw Mrs. DeWitt earlier in the evening, the butler hesitates and admits he saw her going down the stairs, suggesting she may have been in the study. During the questioning, Satterthwaite discovers and pockets a small silver arc of glass from the fireplace.
